turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the International Business Machines Corp. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a challenging quarter as we navigated headwinds across several of our markets. In Q1 2024, We saw a 7% decline in z16 mainframe as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our consulting signings strategy continues to gain traction, with z16 mainframe showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in consulting signings because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from infrastructure validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Arvind. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $53.7 billion, representing a decline of 6% year over year. Our software revenue reached $23.3 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 35.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $8.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Red Hat, we saw 6% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about watsonx AI platform. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On consulting revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position z16 mainframe for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect z16 mainframe to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On z16 mainframe, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Amit. The consulting revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in watsonx?

Thanks, Toni. Great question. On free cash flow, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position watsonx for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect watsonx to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in IBM Cloud?

Thanks, Karl. Great question. On consulting revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position IBM Cloud for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect IBM Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On software revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Red Hat for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Red Hat to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the International Business Machines Corp. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

While we made progress on key strategic initiatives, our financial results were below our expectations. In Q2 2024, We saw a 19% decline in z16 mainframe as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our hybrid cloud strategy strategy continues to gain traction, with z16 mainframe showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in hybrid cloud strategy because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from hybrid cloud validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Arvind. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $101.6 billion, representing a decline of 3% year over year. Our software revenue reached $26.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 28.5%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $26.7 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $3 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Red Hat, we saw 12% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the Red Hat growth trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Amit. The Red Hat growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Shannon. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in z16 mainframe?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On software revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position z16 mainframe for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect z16 mainframe to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the International Business Machines Corp. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q3 2024, Red Hat growth came in below expectations, reflecting ongoing challenges in the current environment. Our consulting signings strategy continues to gain traction, with Red Hat showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in consulting signings because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from hybrid cloud validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Arvind. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $81.8 billion, representing a decline of 3% year over year. Our software revenue reached $46.3 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 42.6%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $10.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $10 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Red Hat, we saw 22% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in watsonx?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On free cash flow, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position watsonx for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect watsonx to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On consulting revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Red Hat for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Red Hat to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Red Hat?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On consulting revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Red Hat for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Red Hat to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the International Business Machines Corp. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q4 2024, We achieved 19% growth in IBM Cloud, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our watsonx AI platform strategy continues to gain traction, with IBM Cloud showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in watsonx AI platform because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from consulting validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Arvind. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $60.4 billion, representing growth of 4% year over year. Our software revenue reached $28.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 44.9%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $29.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $10 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Red Hat, we saw 22% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the Red Hat growth trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On Red Hat growth, we are pleased with the progress on watsonx AI platform and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect watsonx to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to James for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Red Hat?

Sure, Brent. The software revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On Red Hat, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Kash. The consulting revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the International Business Machines Corp. Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q2 2025, We saw 3% growth in z16 mainframe, well above our internal targets. Looking ahead, we see continued strength in demand and expect to deliver above-trend growth next quarter.

Our software revenue reached $47.6 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin was 20.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.2 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $6 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Red Hat, we saw 18% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On watsonx, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Raimo. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the software revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Mark. The software revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Sure, Karl. The consulting revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
